images.caption: Figure 1:Comparison of (a) workflow-based memory systems, where memory operations are governed by predefined retrieval and update pipelines, (b) learning-based memory systems, where memory operations are jointly optimized with downstream agent outcomes, and (c) ourMem-π\pi, which models memory as a generative policyπmem\pi_{\text{mem}}separate from the downstream agent and internalizes reusable experience through offline experience distillation and online adaptation distillation.

Figure 2:Overview ofMem-π\pi.
We train the generative memory policyπmem\pi_{\text{mem}}in two stages.Experience Distillationdistills reusable experience from an offline experience bank via supervised learning.Adaptation Distillationthen optimizesπmem\pi_{\text{mem}}against downstream agent outcomes using decision-content decoupled policy optimization, which pairs abstain and generate rollouts and decomposes the GRPO advantage into decision-level and content-level signals.
Table 2:Ablation results (SR %) onWebArenaandALFWorld. Subscripts show drop from full model.
Figure 4:Abstention rate (line, left axis) and SR improvement over the base agent (bars, right axis) across task-difficulty bins onWebArena.

Figure 6:Case analysis ofMem-π\pi, RAG, and the base agent.
Left: success-set Venn diagram across the three methods, where the colored numbers mark twoMem-π\piwin patterns over RAG: 19 tasks solved only byMem-π\pi, and 10 solved by both the base agent andMem-π\pibut not RAG.
Right: one sampled case for each pattern, comparingMem-π\piand RAG under the same task query.

Figure 8:Sample experience entries drawn from the offline bankℰ\mathcal{E}used to trainMem-π\pi, one per benchmark. Each entry contains a task query (source_trace_goalsin JEF-Hinter(Nekoei et al.,2025)) and the guidance (JEF-Hinter hint) text. ForWebArenaandWorkArena, the bank additionally stores the initial screenshot and a structured grounding description used by the VL memory variant in Section3.3;ALFWorldandLABare text-only environments and have no visual channel.
